bull.b: Sustained strong top-line and EBITDA expansion demonstrates durable demand and execution across the store base. Robust same‑shop sales and transaction growth indicate underlying customer traction, supporting multi-year unit economics and management's raised 2026 revenue and EBITDA guidance.

Material operating cash conversion and a meaningful liquidity cushion provide durable funding for expansion, conversion CapEx, and franchising. Ongoing positive OCF (coverage ~1.35x of net income) supports capital allocation flexibility even if near‑term investment needs persist.

High-performing conversions and record AUVs signal scalable, profitable shop upgrades. Efficient conversion economics and falling average CapEx per shop improve return profiles, enabling faster payback and supporting a repeatable growth playbook for targeted expansion and franchised rollouts.

bear.b: Above-average leverage limits financial flexibility and increases sensitivity to margin shocks or cyclical revenue dips. Although improved from prior years, the debt load constrains optionality for opportunistic investments and raises refinancing and interest‑cost risk if macro rates or cash flow volatility worsen.

Sustained higher coffee and food input costs plus a shift to build‑to‑suit leases that raise occupancy compress shop contribution margins. This structural cost pressure makes achieving long‑term ~30% contribution targets harder and reduces operating leverage as the chain scales.

Declining EBITDA margin despite revenue growth indicates structural mix, cost, or lease impacts that erode profitability. Weaker margin conversion reduces free cash flow scalability (FCF/net income ~0.43) and could limit returns on new openings if cost headwinds persist or mix benefits don't fully materialize.
